fields.description:
## Why
The plan-v2 impact model floors four failure-mode axes at 0.85 (above every blocking threshold) whenever the axis is non-none at ANY grade - the floor is grade-blind. Calibration 3 (task relishable-ammonitic-hoverfly; evidence comment on this story, 2026-07-15) measured the consequence on the plan-v2 segment: 35.5% of ALL blocking findings (807/2271) are floor-driven (would not block without the override), ac_unverifiable alone is 48.9% of the floor-driven set, 52/100 of a classified sample fired at grade=low, and the oracle-kind classification split 56% underspecified_oracle / 37% broken-oracle-shaped / 7% missing_oracle. The dominant floor-driven class is "a check exists, the plan just doesn't spell out the exact command/file/value" - a refinement demand that today auto-blocks with the same force as a genuinely untestable criterion. This story splits the axis so severity follows the actual defect.

## What
Replace the binary-ish ac_unverifiable severity grading with a THREE-value oracle-kind grade in the plan-review verification model, assigned explicitly by the pass-2 verifier (the calibration-3 evidence motivates three, not two - a broken oracle is not a refinement):
- missing_oracle: no verification method exists or could exist as the criterion is phrased (7% of sample) - KEEPS the 0.85 hard floor.
- broken_oracle: a stated proving command/symbol/count is factually wrong - nonexistent path, wrong CLI, contradictory assertion - so the stated verification CANNOT pass (37% of sample) - KEEPS the 0.85 hard floor (the check is protective; these are real plan defects).
- underspecified_oracle: a check exists or is clearly constructible; the finding demands the exact command/file/expected value (56% of sample) - scores BELOW the lowest blocking threshold in force after calibration 3 (0.60), i.e. maps to an impact contribution <= 0.55, so it surfaces and is coached but never auto-blocks.
The verify rubric prompt defines all three grades with one worked example each (drawn from the calibration-3 classified sample). The grade is persisted losslessly in the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar per finding. Because this changes the impact formula's inputs/behavior, IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bumps (plan-v2 -> plan-v3) per ADR 0036 so calibration corpora never pool across the change. The other three override axes (dod_uncertifiable, undecomposed, divergent_implementation) are untouched in this story (their grade mix is unmeasured; calibration 4 scope).

## Scope
- src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py: impact_plan floor logic keyed on the ac_unverifiable grade value (missing/broken keep the floor; underspecified contributes <= 0.55 and never floors).
- The plan-review verification output schema + verify rubric prompt: the closed three-grade vocabulary with definitions and examples; schema validation accepts exactly {none, underspecified_oracle, broken_oracle, missing_oracle} for the axis.
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py: IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump to plan-v3; the grade persists in verification.severity_attributes as today (values change, key stays).
- Unit tests: impact pins for all three grades + none; schema round-trip; sidecar persistence; a regression pin that the other three override axes still floor at any non-none grade.
- docs/plan-review-gate.md: the floor description updated to the graded rule.
- Coordination: story ea39 (R5 kernel sub-answer, epic pastoral-aquatic-viper) also plans a version bump and depends_on this story - if both land close together the bumps compose (v3 then v4), never merge into one ambiguous version.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The verification model accepts exactly the closed grade set {none, underspecified_oracle, broken_oracle, missing_oracle} for ac_unverifiable (schema-validated; anything else rejected), and the verify rubric defines each grade with one worked example.
- [ ] Impact scoring pinned by unit tests: missing_oracle and broken_oracle findings floor at >= 0.85; underspecified_oracle findings score <= 0.55 (below the 0.60 lowest blocking threshold) and never trigger the hard floor; the other three override axes floor unchanged.
- [ ] IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION is plan-v3 in the same change, and the grade value is persisted per finding in the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar, pinned by a test.
- [ ] docs/plan-review-gate.md documents the three-grade rule citing the calibration-3 evidence comment on this story, and the change is MERGED through Gerrit (LLM-Review +1, Verified +1).
